result.stdout:
Description:
  undercutf1

Usage:
  undercutf1 [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose                                  Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  --with-api                                     Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937
  --data-directory <data-directory>              The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --log-directory <log-directory>                The directory to which logs will be written to
  --notify                                       Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  --prefer-ffmpeg                                Prefer the usage of `ffplay` for playing Team Radio on Mac/Linux, instead of afplay/mpg123. `ffplay` is always used on Windows
  --prevent-sleep                                Attempt to prevent the device/display from sleeping whilst the app is running. Intended to help prevent accidental stream disconnection due to inactivity on your device.
  --force-graphics-protocol <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>  Forces the usage of a particular graphics protocol.
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
  --version                                      Show version information

Commands:
  import <year>                     Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
                                    The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using undercutf1.
  info                              Prints diagnostics about undercutf1, and the terminal in the command is run in.
  image <file> <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>  Displays the image from the provided filepath in the terminal, using the appropiate graphics protocol.
  login                             Login to your Formula 1 account to unlock all data feeds (like the driver tracker).
  logout                            Logout of your Formula 1 account.

commands.payload:
Description:
  undercutf1

Usage:
  undercutf1 [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose                                  Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  --with-api                                     Whether the API endpoint should be exposed at http://localhost:61937
  --data-directory <data-directory>              The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --log-directory <log-directory>                The directory to which logs will be written to
  --notify                                       Whether audible BELs are sent to your terminal when new race control messages are received
  --prefer-ffmpeg                                Prefer the usage of `ffplay` for playing Team Radio on Mac/Linux, instead of afplay/mpg123. `ffplay` is always used on Windows
  --prevent-sleep                                Attempt to prevent the device/display from sleeping whilst the app is running. Intended to help prevent accidental stream disconnection due to inactivity on your device.
  --force-graphics-protocol <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>  Forces the usage of a particular graphics protocol.
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
  --version                                      Show version information

Commands:
  import <year>                     Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
                                    The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using undercutf1.
  info                              Prints diagnostics about undercutf1, and the terminal in the command is run in.
  image <file> <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>  Displays the image from the provided filepath in the terminal, using the appropiate graphics protocol.
  login                             Login to your Formula 1 account to unlock all data feeds (like the driver tracker).
  logout                            Logout of your Formula 1 account.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Displays the image from the provided filepath in the terminal, using the appropiate graphics protocol.

Usage:
  undercutf1 image <file> <The graphics protocol to use> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>
  <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Displays the image from the provided filepath in the terminal, using the appropiate graphics protocol.

Usage:
  undercutf1 image <file> <The graphics protocol to use> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>
  <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
  The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using undercutf1.

Usage:
  undercutf1 import <year> [options]

Arguments:
  <year>  The year the meeting took place.

Options:
  -m, --meeting, --meeting-key <meeting-key>  The Meeting Key of the session to import. If not provided, all meetings in the year will be listed.
  -s, --session, --session-key <session-key>  The Session Key of the session inside the selected meeting to import. If not provided, all sessions in the provided meeting will be listed.
  --data-directory <data-directory>           The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --log-directory <log-directory>             The directory to which logs will be written to
  -v, --verbose                               Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Imports data from the F1 Live Timing API, if you have missed recording a session live. 
  The data is imported in a format that can be replayed in real-time using undercutf1.

Usage:
  undercutf1 import <year> [options]

Arguments:
  <year>  The year the meeting took place.

Options:
  -m, --meeting, --meeting-key <meeting-key>  The Meeting Key of the session to import. If not provided, all meetings in the year will be listed.
  -s, --session, --session-key <session-key>  The Session Key of the session inside the selected meeting to import. If not provided, all sessions in the provided meeting will be listed.
  --data-directory <data-directory>           The directory to which timing data will be read from and written to
  --log-directory <log-directory>             The directory to which logs will be written to
  -v, --verbose                               Whether verbose logging should be enabled
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

  --log-directory <log-directory>                The directory to which logs will be written to

  --prevent-sleep                                Attempt to prevent the device/display from sleeping whilst the app is running. Intended to help prevent accidental stream disconnection due to inactivity on your device.
  --force-graphics-protocol <iTerm|Kitty|Sixel>  Forces the usage of a particular graphics protocol.
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
